# Azure Static Web Apps Migration Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Migrate ulostudios.com from Vercel to Azure Static Web Apps (Free tier) with automatic GitHub deploys and custom domain.

**Architecture:** Next.js 16 app hosted on Azure Static Web Apps in hybrid mode — static pages served from Azure CDN, `/api/contact` route as a managed Azure Function. GitHub Actions CI/CD auto-generated by Azure on resource creation. Custom domain `ulostudios.com` with Azure-managed SSL.

**Tech Stack:** Azure Static Web Apps (Free), Azure CLI, GitHub Actions, Next.js 16, gh CLI

## Global Constraints

- Resource group: `ulostudios` (existing, `westus`)
- SWA region: `westus2` (closest region SWA supports to `westus`)
- GitHub repo: `https://github.com/uofuonye/ulostudios`
- Branch: `main`
- Domain: `ulostudios.com`
- Env vars required: `RESEND_API_KEY`, `RESEND_FROM_EMAIL`, `CONTACT_TO_EMAIL`
- Values from: `/Users/galacticuche/Desktop/Workspace/ulostudios/.env.local`

---

### Task 1: Create Azure Static Web Apps resource

**Files:**
- Auto-created by Azure: `.github/workflows/azure-static-web-apps-*.yml`

**Interfaces:**
- Produces: SWA resource named `ulostudios-swa`, deployment token stored as GitHub secret

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the SWA resource linked to GitHub**

```bash
az staticwebapp create \
  --name ulostudios-swa \
  --resource-group ulostudios \
  --source https://github.com/uofuonye/ulostudios \
  --location "westus2" \
  --branch main \
  --app-location "/" \
  --output-location ".next" \
  --sku Free \
  --token $(gh auth token)
```

Expected output: JSON with `"defaultHostname": "*.azurestaticapps.net"` and `"repositoryUrl"`. Note the `defaultHostname` value.

- [ ] **Step 2: Confirm resource was created**

```bash
az staticwebapp show \
  --name ulostudios-swa \
  --resource-group ulostudios \
  --query "{hostname:defaultHostname, state:repositoryToken}" \
  --output table
```

Expected: hostname ending in `.azurestaticapps.net` is shown.

---

### Task 2: Configure environment variables

**Files:** None (stored in Azure portal/settings only)

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: SWA resource `ulostudios-swa` from Task 1
- Produces: Env vars available to Next.js API route at runtime

- [ ] **Step 1: Set all three environment variables**

Read values from `.env.local` and apply (replace `<VALUE>` with actual values from `.env.local`):

```bash
az staticwebapp appsettings set \
  --name ulostudios-swa \
  --resource-group ulostudios \
  --setting-names \
    RESEND_API_KEY="<value from .env.local>" \
    RESEND_FROM_EMAIL="info@ulostudios.com" \
    CONTACT_TO_EMAIL="info@ulostudios.com"
```

Expected output: JSON list of the three settings without values (Azure masks them).

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify settings are stored**

```bash
az staticwebapp appsettings list \
  --name ulostudios-swa \
  --resource-group ulostudios \
  --output table
```

Expected: Table showing `RESEND_API_KEY`, `RESEND_FROM_EMAIL`, `CONTACT_TO_EMAIL`.

---

### Task 3: Verify initial deployment

**Files:** None

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: GitHub Actions workflow from Task 1, env vars from Task 2
- Produces: Working site on default `*.azurestaticapps.net` URL

- [ ] **Step 1: Check GitHub Actions workflow was committed to repo**

```bash
git -C /Users/galacticuche/Desktop/Workspace/ulostudios pull origin main && \
ls /Users/galacticuche/Desktop/Workspace/ulostudios/.github/workflows/
```

Expected: A file named `azure-static-web-apps-*.yml` exists.

- [ ] **Step 2: Check GitHub Actions run status**

```bash
gh run list --repo uofuonye/ulostudios --limit 3
```

Expected: A run named "Azure Static Web Apps CI/CD" — wait for it to show `completed` / `success`.

- [ ] **Step 3: If run is still in progress, wait**

```bash
gh run watch --repo uofuonye/ulostudios
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Get the default hostname and test it**

```bash
az staticwebapp show \
  --name ulostudios-swa \
  --resource-group ulostudios \
  --query "defaultHostname" \
  --output tsv
```

Open the URL in a browser and verify:
- Home page loads
- Navigation works
- Contact form submits without errors (test with a real email)

---

### Task 4: Configure custom domain

**Files:** None

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: SWA resource from Task 1
- Produces: `ulostudios.com` validation records for DNS update

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the apex domain to SWA**

```bash
az staticwebapp hostname set \
  --name ulostudios-swa \
  --resource-group ulostudios \
  --hostname ulostudios.com
```

Expected: JSON with `"validationMethod"` and either a `TXT` or `CNAME` record value.

- [ ] **Step 2: Add the www subdomain**

```bash
az staticwebapp hostname set \
  --name ulostudios-swa \
  --resource-group ulostudios \
  --hostname www.ulostudios.com
```

Expected: CNAME record value pointing to `*.azurestaticapps.net`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Get validation record details**

```bash
az staticwebapp hostname list \
  --name ulostudios-swa \
  --resource-group ulostudios \
  --output table
```

Note the `validationToken` and `hostname` values — these are the DNS records to add at the registrar.

- [ ] **Step 4: Provide DNS instructions to user**

Share the DNS records from Step 3 with the user so they can update their registrar. The records needed are typically:
- For `ulostudios.com` (apex): A record or ALIAS/ANAME pointing to Azure, plus a TXT record for domain verification
- For `www.ulostudios.com`: CNAME pointing to the `*.azurestaticapps.net` hostname

---

### Task 5: DNS cutover and Vercel cleanup

**Files:** None

- [ ] **Step 1: User updates DNS at registrar** (user action — paste the records from Task 4 Step 3)

- [ ] **Step 2: Monitor DNS propagation**

```bash
watch -n 30 "dig ulostudios.com +short && dig www.ulostudios.com +short"
```

Wait until both resolve to Azure IP addresses (not Vercel `76.76.21.*` addresses).

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify SSL certificate is provisioned**

```bash
az staticwebapp hostname list \
  --name ulostudios-swa \
  --resource-group ulostudios \
  --query "[].{hostname:name, status:status}" \
  --output table
```

Expected: Both hostnames show `Validated`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Final end-to-end test on custom domain**

Open `https://ulostudios.com` and verify:
- HTTPS works (green padlock)
- All pages load (/, /about, /portfolio, /contact, /yumble)
- Contact form sends email successfully

- [ ] **Step 5: Remove project from Vercel** (only after Step 4 confirms everything works)

Go to Vercel dashboard → ulostudios project → Settings → Delete Project.
